description_zh: 一種自主吞嚥技術，旨在吞嚥前後閉合聲帶以防止誤吸。患者屏住呼吸、吞嚥，然後立即咳嗽以清除喉前庭殘留物。
target_population: Individuals with reduced airway closure, risk of aspiration during the swallow
activities: Inhale and hold breath tightly
Swallow while continuing to hold breath
Cough immediately after swallowing to clear airway
evidence_basis: Well-established technique with evidence for reducing aspiration in specific populations
reference: Martin, B. J., Logemann, J. A., Shaker, R., & Dodds, W. J. (1993). Normal laryngeal valving patterns during three breath-hold maneuvers: A pilot investigation. Dysphagia, 8(1), 11-20. https://doi.org/10.1007/BF01351472
procedure: Confirm patient is alert, able to follow multi-step commands, and has adequate respiratory function for breath-holding
Explain 3-step sequence: take breath and hold it, swallow while holding breath, then cough immediately after swallowing
Practice breath-hold in isolation for 3-5 seconds to ensure patient can achieve and sustain airway closure
Practice complete dry swallow sequence (breath-hold, swallow, cough) without bolus until patient demonstrates mastery
Introduce small bolus (3-5ml) and practice full sequence with food/liquid to apply technique functionally
Monitor for signs of laryngeal penetration or aspiration (throat clearing, cough, wet vocal quality) during trials
Complete 5-10 practice trials per session with rest breaks to prevent fatigue and maintain technique quality
Schedule instrumental reassessment (FEES or VFSS) after 4-6 weeks of practice to document reduced aspiration
case_scenario_zh: 鄭先生，52歲男士，喉癌部分切除術後，吞嚥時有誤吸風險。治療師教導聲門上吞嚥法四步驟：深吸氣、閉氣屏住、吞嚥、吞嚥後立即咳嗽清喉。先以空吞嚥練習動作協調，熟練後在稀液體進食時應用。治療師以內視鏡確認聲門閉合時機，指導鄭先生感受閉氣的肌肉感覺，確保在正確時機完成閉合動作，有效防止吞嚥時誤吸。

description_en: A compensatory technique that increases posterior tongue base retraction and pharyngeal pressure during swallowing. Patients are instructed to squeeze hard with all swallowing muscles to improve bolus clearance and reduce pharyngeal residue.
description_zh: 一種代償技術，增加吞嚥時舌根後縮和咽部壓力。指導患者用力收縮所有吞嚥肌肉，以改善食團清除並減少咽部殘留。
target_population: Reduced tongue base retraction, pharyngeal residue, weak pharyngeal contraction
activities: Instruct patient to swallow as hard as possible
Squeeze all muscles in throat and mouth during swallow
Practice with saliva first, then progress to food/liquid
evidence_basis: Evidence supports increased pharyngeal pressures and tongue base movement
reference: Hind, J. A., Nicosia, M. A., Roecker, E. B., Carnes, M. L., & Robbins, J. (2001). Comparison of effortful and noneffortful swallows in healthy middle-aged and older adults. Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation, 82(12), 1661-1665. https://doi.org/10.1053/apmr.2001.28006
procedure: Explain rationale: increased effort recruits more muscle activity to improve bolus clearance and reduce residue
Demonstrate maximum squeeze maneuver by showing exaggerated facial/neck tension during swallow
Begin with dry swallows, asking patient to rate effort on 1-10 scale and aim for 8-10 effort level
Introduce 5ml bolus and instruct patient to swallow with hard squeeze, thinking squeeze all the way down
Complete 3 sets of 5 effortful swallows per session with 30-second rest between sets
Gradually advance bolus size and texture as patient maintains effortful technique with smaller volumes
Monitor for excessive tension or fatigue; cue patient to focus effort on pharyngeal squeeze not neck muscles
Reassess pharyngeal residue using FEES or VFSS after 6-8 weeks to document improved clearance
case_scenario_zh: 吳女士，64歲女士，鼻咽癌放療後咽部收縮力減弱，吞嚥後食物殘留咽部。治療師教導用力吞嚥法：吞嚥時全力收縮所有口腔和咽部肌肉，如同『用盡全力擠壓』食物向下。先練習空吞嚥感受肌肉用力感，再在進食糊狀食物時應用。治療師以手觸碰吳女士的頸部感受咽部收縮力度，提供即時觸覺反饋，每節課練習20次，配合螢光透視追蹤咽部殘留改善情況。

description_en: An exercise designed to strengthen pharyngeal wall muscles by having the patient hold their tongue between their teeth while swallowing. This prevents tongue base retraction and increases the workload on the pharyngeal constrictors.
description_zh: 通過讓患者在吞嚥時將舌頭置於牙齒之間，以強化咽壁肌肉的運動。這防止舌根後縮，增加咽收縮肌的工作負荷。
target_population: Reduced pharyngeal wall movement, pharyngeal residue in valleculae or pyriform sinuses
activities: Hold tongue gently between front teeth
Attempt to swallow saliva while maintaining tongue position
Complete 10 repetitions, 3 times daily (exercise only, not for food/liquid)
evidence_basis: Evidence shows increased pharyngeal wall activation; limited evidence for functional outcomes
reference: Fujiu, M., & Logemann, J. A. (1996). Effect of a tongue-holding maneuver on posterior pharyngeal wall movement during deglutition. American Journal of Speech-Language Pathology, 5(1), 23-30. https://doi.org/10.1044/1058-0360.0501.23
procedure: Explain rationale: holding tongue forward during swallow strengthens posterior pharyngeal wall contraction
Note contraindications: tongue weakness, reduced base of tongue retraction, post-glossectomy patients
Practice tongue protrusion: patient gently bites tongue between teeth to maintain forward position
Perform dry swallows with tongue held gently between teeth, ensuring patient feels pharyngeal contraction
Complete 5-10 swallows per set for 2-3 sets per session, with rest breaks to minimize tongue fatigue
Monitor for tongue discomfort, soreness, or inability to maintain position; reduce repetitions if needed
Emphasize this is exercise only, not a compensatory strategy - never use with food or liquid
Reassess with FEES or VFSS after 6-8 weeks of strengthening to evaluate improved pharyngeal wall movement
case_scenario_zh: 黃先生，59歲男士，中風後咽後壁移動減少，吞嚥效率低。治療師教導正子吞嚥法：將舌尖輕輕咬住於門牙之間，保持舌頭前置的狀態下進行空吞嚥。此練習強化咽後壁向前收縮的力度。每節課練習10次空吞嚥，每週三節，持續六週。治療師提醒此為強化練習而非代償策略，實際進食時不應採用此姿勢，以免增加殘留。

description_en: A series of exercises targeting pharyngeal musculature to improve swallow strength and coordination. Includes resistance exercises, isometric holds, and functional swallowing tasks designed to enhance pharyngeal constriction and bolus clearance.
description_zh: 針對咽部肌肉組織的系列訓練，改善吞嚥力量和協調性。包括阻力訓練、等長收縮及功能性吞嚥任務，旨在增強咽部收縮和食團清除能力。
target_population: Pharyngeal weakness, reduced pharyngeal constriction, post-radiation therapy
activities: Gargle exercises with sustained vocalization
Yawn and hold to stretch pharyngeal muscles
Progressive resistance swallowing with varied bolus sizes
evidence_basis: Emerging evidence for pharyngeal strengthening protocols; more research needed
reference: Shaker, R., Easterling, C., Kern, M., Nitschke, T., Massey, B., Daniels, S., ... & Dikeman, K. (2002). Rehabilitation of swallowing by exercise in tube-fed patients with pharyngeal dysphagia secondary to abnormal UES opening. Gastroenterology, 122(5), 1314-1321. https://doi.org/10.1053/gast.2002.32999
procedure: Complete instrumental assessment (FEES or VFSS) to identify specific pharyngeal weakness (base of tongue, pharyngeal wall, UES opening)
Select targeted exercises based on deficit: Masako for pharyngeal wall, effortful swallow for BOT, Mendelsohn for UES
Establish baseline measures using validated tools (PAS score, residue rating, pharyngeal constriction measures)
Introduce exercises as dry swallow practice only, ensuring patient understands proper technique and muscle target
Prescribe home exercise programme: 3 sets of 10 repetitions twice daily (morning and evening) for consistency
Monitor for muscle fatigue, soreness, or decreased performance; adjust intensity or repetitions as needed
Schedule instrumental reassessment every 6-8 weeks to measure physiological change and guide programme progression
Modify exercise programme based on reassessment findings: advance intensity, change exercises, or introduce functional practice
case_scenario_zh: 陳女士，56歲女士，頭頸癌放療後咽部肌肉纖維化，吞嚥費力且有殘留。治療師採用咽部強化訓練，包括：用力吞嚥練習、Masako練習、假聲發音練習（強化咽部閉合）及Shaker抬頭練習，每天兩組各10次。治療師以螢光透視評估基線咽部收縮幅度，每四週複評，根據進展調整練習強度。配合食物質感調整確保安全進食，目標是逐步恢復正常飲食。

description_en: A systematic approach to weaning children from tube feeding developed in Graz, Austria. Emphasizes hunger provocation, parent empowerment, and multidisciplinary support to transition from enteral to oral feeding while monitoring nutritional status and growth.
description_zh: 一種系統化的兒童管飼脫離方法，源自奧地利格拉茨。強調飢餓刺激、家長賦能及多學科支持，在監測營養狀況和成長的同時，從腸道餵養過渡到口腔餵養。
target_population: Children dependent on tube feeding with medical stability for oral transition
activities: Gradual reduction of tube feeding volume to stimulate hunger
Parent coaching on responsive feeding and managing tube dependency
Multidisciplinary monitoring of weight, hydration, and oral intake
evidence_basis: Strong evidence from multiple centers; high success rates with structured protocol
reference: Dunitz-Scheer, M., Levine, A., Roth, Y., Kratky, E., Beckenbach, H., Kuschnig, A., ... & Scheer, P. J. (2009). Prevention and treatment of tube dependency in infancy and early childhood. ICAN: Infant, Child, & Adolescent Nutrition, 1(2), 73-82. https://doi.org/10.1177/1941406409333209
procedure: Obtain comprehensive history: tube duration, previous oral feeding attempts, medical comorbidities, developmental level
Assess oral feeding potential: oral motor skills, swallow safety, hunger cues, behavioral readiness for oral intake
Establish baseline oral intake diary for 3-5 days: document all oral attempts, volume, textures, behaviors
Implement hunger-based feeding schedule: reduce tube feeding volume by 10-20% to create mild hunger without malnutrition
Offer oral feeding in pressure-free, play-based environment during peak hunger times (before scheduled tube feeds)
Gradually shift caloric balance from tube to oral: increase oral opportunities and decrease tube volumes systematically
Monitor weight weekly with dietitian oversight; ensure weight remains stable or shows controlled growth during weaning
Discharge tube feeding when child sustains full oral nutrition for 2 consecutive weeks with adequate growth trajectory
case_scenario_zh: 小恆，2歲男孩，因早產和手術長期靠鼻胃管餵食，對口腔進食毫無興趣。治療師採用格拉茨管飼脫離模型，與醫療團隊確認他已具備醫療穩定性，開始系統性減少管飼量以建立飢餓感。每天在固定時間安排輕鬆的口腔進食機會，不施壓，讓他自主探索食物。治療師密切監察體重，家長每天記錄口腔進食量，逐步增加口腔份量同時相應減少管飼，目標在八至十二週內完全脫離鼻胃管。

name_zh: 應用行為分析進食介入
description_en: Uses principles of Applied Behaviour Analysis to address severe feeding difficulties, particularly food refusal and selectivity. Employs systematic reinforcement, escape extinction, and shaping procedures to expand food repertoire and improve mealtime behaviour.
description_zh: 運用應用行為分析原理處理嚴重進食困難，特別是拒食和選擇性進食。採用系統化強化、逃避消退及塑造程序，擴展食物接受範圍並改善進餐行為。
target_population: Severe food refusal, highly selective eating, autism spectrum disorder with feeding issues
activities: Systematic desensitization to new foods using small steps
Positive reinforcement for food acceptance and tasting
Escape extinction to reduce food refusal behaviours
evidence_basis: Strong empirical support for improving food acceptance in severe feeding disorders
reference: Sharp, W. G., Jaquess, D. L., Morton, J. F., & Herzinger, C. V. (2010). Pediatric feeding disorders: A quantitative synthesis of treatment outcomes. Clinical Child and Family Psychology Review, 13(4), 348-365. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10567-010-0079-7
procedure: Conduct comprehensive feeding assessment and functional behavior analysis: ABC data (antecedent, behavior, consequence)
Complete preference assessment to identify high-value reinforcers (toys, praise, activities) for positive feeding behaviors
Define target food and create exposure hierarchy: touching food, smelling, licking, biting, chewing, swallowing
Implement systematic desensitization with positive reinforcement: reward approximations toward target eating behavior
Use extinction procedures for maladaptive behaviors (food refusal, tantrums, mouth packing): withhold reinforcement consistently
Advance through hierarchy when child accepts current step 3 consecutive times at 80% success rate
Generalize skills across people (parents, siblings, teachers) and settings (home, school, restaurants) systematically
Develop maintenance plan: fading reinforcement schedule, monitoring protocols, booster session schedule for setbacks
case_scenario_zh: 軒仔，4歲男孩，患自閉症，嚴重食物拒絕，只接受三種食物，任何新食物出現即大哭。治療師採用ABA進食介入，先進行偏好評估確認最強強化物，制定食物接受層次的行為目標。以系統脫敏配合正強化：容忍食物在盤中可獲最愛玩具30秒，達標後才進至觸碰食物。治療師以DTT格式進行，精確記錄每個嘗試的反應數據，每週分析進展並調整介入計劃。

description_en: A comprehensive oral motor intervention program designed to improve feeding skills in infants and young children. Focuses on jaw, lip, and tongue exercises combined with sensory techniques to enhance oral motor control for safe and efficient feeding.
description_zh: 一套全面的口腔運動介入方案，旨在改善嬰幼兒的進食技能。專注於下顎、嘴唇和舌頭運動，結合感覺技術，以增強安全有效進食的口腔運動控制。
target_population: Infants and children with oral motor delays, hypotonia, or coordination difficulties
activities: Systematic jaw grading exercises for controlled bite
Lip and tongue strengthening activities
Sensory integration techniques for oral acceptance
evidence_basis: Clinical support; limited controlled studies on functional feeding outcomes
reference: Beckman, D. A. (2013). Beckman oral motor assessment and intervention. Beckman & Associates.
procedure: Complete formal Beckman Oral Motor Assessment to identify specific deficits in tone, range, strength, and function
Obtain informed consent explaining hands-on oral motor facilitation approach and therapeutic handling techniques
Cheek and lip stretches: gentle lateral and circular stretches to improve tone and range of motion
Jaw activation: provide graded upward pressure under chin during mouth opening to facilitate jaw stability and grading
Tongue activation: use tongue depressor to provide resistance for tongue elevation, lateralization, and protrusion
Lip closure work: facilitate lip rounding and closure using gentle touch cues and resistance at lip corners
Complete 5-10 assisted repetitions per oral motor component with careful monitoring of infant tolerance
Document infant responses, tolerance, and carryover to feeding; reassess monthly to track progress and adjust techniques
case_scenario_zh: 安安，2歲半女孩，唐氏綜合症，口腔低張力，流口水，咀嚼能力弱。治療師採用貝克曼口腔運動方案，以系統性被動輔助運動評估口腔各部位的抵抗力和運動範圍，再針對薄弱部位進行輔助主動運動：下頜上下運動、嘴唇閉合牽拉、臉頰内外推壓、舌頭前後側移。每節課15至20分鐘，每週兩節，教導家長在家每天進行一次，配合食物質感調整促進咀嚼發展。

description_en: A child-centered feeding approach that emphasizes recognizing and responding appropriately to a child's hunger and satiety cues. Promotes a positive feeding relationship by balancing parental structure with child autonomy, reducing mealtime conflict and improving intake.
description_zh: 以兒童為中心的餵養方法，強調識別並適當回應兒童的飢餓和飽足信號。通過平衡家長結構與兒童自主性，促進正向餵養關係，減少進餐衝突並改善攝入。
target_population: General feeding difficulties, poor appetite, parent-child feeding conflicts
activities: Teach parents to recognize infant/child hunger and fullness cues
Practice responsive prompting without pressure or coercion
Establish predictable meal structure while respecting child autonomy
evidence_basis: Strong theoretical foundation and growing evidence base for preventing and treating feeding problems
reference: Black, M. M., & Aboud, F. E. (2011). Responsive feeding is embedded in a theoretical framework of responsive parenting. The Journal of Nutrition, 141(3), 490-494. https://doi.org/10.3945/jn.110.129973
procedure: Observe full mealtime interaction to identify non-responsive patterns (pressure, distraction, disengagement, control battles)
Educate caregivers on responsive feeding principles: division of responsibility, reciprocal interaction, attunement to cues
Address mealtime structure: regular schedule, appropriate duration, minimizing distractions to support engagement
Teach recognition of infant/child hunger and satiety cues across developmental stages (early, active, late cues)
Guide caregivers to offer food without pressure: present, encourage, accept refusal, trust child appetite regulation
Provide in-session coaching during mealtimes with real-time feedback on responsive vs. non-responsive interactions
Systematically reduce non-responsive feeding strategies: coaxing, forcing, bribing, distracting during spoon delivery
Reassess feeding relationship and mealtime quality monthly using validated measures and caregiver report
case_scenario_zh: 小桃，3歲女孩，食慾差，進食量少，家長焦慮而強迫進食，每餐哭鬧。治療師採用回應式餵養治療，教導家長辨認並回應小桃的飢餓和飽足信號，停止強迫進食行為。建立規律進食時間表，每隔2.5至3小時提供餐食，零食時間固定。治療師與家長分析進食錄像，辨認互動中的緊張時刻，提供具體回應策略，逐步重建輕鬆正向的進食關係。

description_en: A systematic approach to expanding food variety through repeated neutral exposure to new tastes and textures. Based on the principle that familiarity increases acceptance, children are exposed to target foods multiple times without pressure to eat, gradually reducing neophobia.
description_zh: 通過重複中性接觸新味道和質感，系統化擴展食物多樣性的方法。基於熟悉度提升接受度的原理，兒童在無進食壓力下多次接觸目標食物，逐漸減少新食物恐懼。
target_population: Food neophobia, selective eating, limited food variety
activities: Present new food 10-15 times without pressure to eat
Encourage exploration through touch, smell, and small tastes
Track exposure frequency and acceptance progression
evidence_basis: Well-supported by research on repeated exposure and food acceptance in children
reference: Cooke, L. (2007). The importance of exposure for healthy eating in childhood: A review. Journal of Human Nutrition and Dietetics, 20(4), 294-301. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-277X.2007.00804.x
procedure: Obtain detailed food history: accepted foods, rejected foods, level of exposure tolerance, sensory patterns
Explain systematic exposure rationale to family: repeated neutral exposures without eating pressure reduce food neophobia
Develop food hierarchy ranking target foods from least to most challenging based on sensory properties
Begin with food play and exploration activities without any expectation of eating (touching, smelling, playing)
Progress to near-mouth exposure: food on plate, touching lips, kissing food, licking without swallowing
Introduce taste exposure: small lick or taste, spit out allowed, no pressure to swallow initially
Advance through hierarchy with 3-5 neutral exposures per step before progressing to next level
Set goal of 10-15 total exposures per target food before expecting acceptance; track exposure frequency and responses
case_scenario_zh: 俊生，5歲男孩，對新食物極度抗拒，食物種類少於15種。治療師採用味覺暴露治療，制定系統性接觸計劃：每週引入一種新食物，以遊戲方式反覆接觸，不強迫進食。活動包括：觀察食物顏色形狀、以工具觸碰、聞氣味、親吻食物、舔一下。治療師記錄每次接觸的反應，通常需要10至15次接觸後才建立接受，強調過程輕鬆無壓力，家長在家複製同樣活動。

description_en: An intervention approach addressing feeding difficulties related to sensory processing challenges. Uses graded sensory exposure, environmental modifications, and desensitization techniques to help children tolerate and accept a wider variety of food textures, temperatures, and flavors.
description_zh: 針對感覺處理挑戰相關進食困難的介入方法。使用漸進式感覺暴露、環境調整及脫敏技術，幫助兒童耐受並接受更廣泛的食物質感、溫度和味道。
target_population: Sensory processing disorder, tactile defensiveness, extreme texture selectivity
activities: Graded exposure to varied food textures (smooth to mixed to lumpy)
Oral sensory play activities outside of mealtimes
Environmental adaptations to reduce sensory overload during meals
evidence_basis: Clinical support and case studies; emerging controlled research
reference: Engel-Yeger, B., Hardal-Nasser, R., & Gal, E. (2011). Sensory processing dysfunctions as expressed among children with different severities of intellectual developmental disabilities. Research in Developmental Disabilities, 32(5), 1770-1775. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ridd.2011.03.005
procedure: Complete comprehensive sensory processing assessment (e.g., Sensory Profile, SPM) to identify patterns and preferences
Classify sensory pattern: hyper-responsive (defensive, avoidant) vs. hypo-responsive (seeking, under-reactive) to guide intervention
Implement sensory desensitization programme starting with non-oral activities: messy play, tactile bins, brushing protocol
Introduce sensory oral stimulation programme: oral massage, vibration, varied textures on face and in mouth (non-food)
Grade food introduction from least to most challenging sensorially: predictable to varied texture, bland to strong flavor
Structure mealtimes with predictable sensory routines: same place, utensils, presentation style to reduce sensory load
Coach caregivers in sensory-informed feeding strategies: pacing, environmental modifications, sensory breaks during meals
Reassess sensory patterns and feeding progress every 6-8 weeks; adjust intervention based on emerging tolerance patterns
case_scenario_zh: 嘉嘉，2歲女孩，感覺處理障礙，口腔觸覺防禦強，拒絕所有糊狀和濕滑質感的食物，只接受乾脆食物。治療師採用感覺為本進食介入，先進行全身感覺脫敏活動如深壓和關節本體感覺輸入，降低整體感覺防禦水平。再針對口腔進行系統性脫敏：從手部觸碰糊狀物料，到面部，再到嘴唇外，最後口腔內。配合職業治療師協作，制定家庭感覺飲食計劃，每天進行感覺準備活動後再嘗試進食。

description_en: Sketch and Speak is an expository strategy intervention developed by Teresa Ukrainetz (Utah State University) for school-age students with language-related learning disabilities. Students learn to take quick pictographic notes from informational texts (Sketch), then verbally formulate and rehearse individual sentences and whole oral reports using their sketches (Speak). The intervention is grounded in Ukrainetz's contextualized skill framework: skills are explicitly taught and scaffolded within purposeful communicative activities. Session 1 focuses on pictograph note-taking paragraph by paragraph; Session 2 converts sketches to oral bullet-point sentences, then to a full oral report. Evidence supports improved note quality, oral reporting, and strategy awareness.
description_zh: 素描說話法由美國猶他州立大學Teresa Ukrainetz教授發展，專為有語言相關學習障礙的學齡學生而設。學生學習以快速圖畫筆記（素描）從資訊性文本中提取重點，再利用圖畫提示口頭組織和演練句子及完整口頭報告（說話）。介入以Ukrainetz的情境化技能框架為基礎：在有目的的溝通活動中明確教導並鷹架技能。第一節課以段落為單位進行圖畫筆記；第二節課將圖畫轉換為口頭要點句子，再組合成完整口頭報告。研究顯示學生在筆記質素、口頭報告能力及策略意識方面均有顯著改善。
target_population: School-age children and adolescents (approx. ages 8–16) with developmental language disorder, language-related learning disabilities, or DLD with literacy difficulties
activities: Pictograph (sketch) note-taking from expository paragraphs
Oral sentence formulation from sketches ('Say it simply')
Verbal rehearsal and full oral report delivery
Text structure analysis (description, sequence, compare-contrast, cause-effect, problem-solution)
Graphic organiser use for main idea and supporting details
evidence_basis: RCT (Ukrainetz, 2019; n=44); multiple single-case experiments; replicated across school-age and adolescent populations including autism and telepractice contexts
reference:
Ukrainetz, T. A. (2019). Sketch and Speak: An expository intervention using note-taking and oral practice for children with language-related learning disabilities. Language, Speech, and Hearing Services in Schools, 50(1), 53–70. https://doi.org/10.1044/2018_LSHSS-18-0047

Ukrainetz, T. A. (2024). Evidence-based expository intervention: A tutorial for speech-language pathologists. American Journal of Speech-Language Pathology, 33(2), 654–675. https://doi.org/10.1044/2023_AJSLP-23-00036
procedure: Select a short informational text (1–3 paragraphs) matched to the student's reading level and curriculum topic.
Introduce the text topic and activate background knowledge; discuss the expository text structure (e.g., description, cause-effect).

Session 1 – Sketch: Read one paragraph aloud together. Ask the student to identify the key idea and draw a quick, simple sketch ('just enough to remember — not an art project'). Repeat paragraph by paragraph.
Review each sketch: prompt the student to explain it with a full, grammatical sentence using the cue 'Say it simply'. Provide sentence starters or recasts as needed.
Have the student give a full oral retell of the text using only their sketches as prompts.
Session 2 – Speak: Student converts each sketch to a spoken bullet-point sentence, then writes it as a brief written note.
Student practises delivering the full oral report from written notes, then from sketches only.
Provide explicit feedback on accuracy, completeness, and fluency of the oral report.
Progress to longer texts, independent note-taking without scaffolding, and eventually written reports.
Measure progress via note quality rubric, holistic oral report rating, and strategy awareness self-report.
case_scenario_zh: 俊熙，11歲男生，就讀小五，有發展性語言障礙及讀寫困難，科學課需要口頭報告但每次都說不清楚，內容零散沒有重點。治療師採用素描說話法，選取一篇關於蝴蝶生命週期的三段課文。第一節課，治療師與俊熙逐段閱讀，每段完成後請他用簡單線條畫出最重要的一個意思，例如用「蛋→毛蟲→蛹→蝴蝶」的圖示記錄變化過程。畫好後治療師提示「用一句話告訴我這幅畫的意思」，俊熙說出「蝴蝶先由蛋孵出來變成毛蟲」，治療師以擴展技術示範更完整的句子。第二節課，俊熙用三幅圖畫逐一說出句子，再組合成完整口頭報告，最後在無圖畫提示下嘗試獨立報告。六節課後俊熙能有條理地講出四段重點，口頭報告的組織結構和流暢度均有明顯提升。

description_en: Morphological awareness intervention explicitly teaches students to identify, analyse, and manipulate morphemes — the smallest meaningful units of language (e.g., roots, prefixes, suffixes, inflections). Children with DLD, dyslexia, or language-based learning disabilities often have poor morphological awareness despite strong phonological awareness instruction, limiting vocabulary growth, reading comprehension, and spelling. Instruction typically covers derivational morphology (un-, re-, -ful, -tion) and inflectional morphology (-ed, -ing, -s). Activities include word sorting by morpheme families, word matrix exercises, morpheme mapping, and generative vocabulary tasks. Meta-analytic evidence (Goodwin & Ahn 2010, k=17, d=0.77) confirms moderate-to-large effects for children with language and reading difficulties. Colenbrander et al. (2024, JSHL) replicated these findings with a school-age DLD population in a randomised design, showing gains transferring to untreated vocabulary and reading comprehension.
description_zh: 詞素意識介入透過明確教導學生識別、分析及操控詞素（語言中最小的意義單位，如詞根、前綴、後綴及屈折變化），提升詞彙量、閱讀理解及拼寫能力。患有發展性語言障礙、讀寫障礙或語言學習障礙的兒童，其詞素意識往往較弱,即使已接受音素意識訓練亦未見改善,因此需要額外的直接教導。介入內容涵蓋派生詞素（如un-、re-、-ful、-tion）及屈折詞素（如-ed、-ing、-s）,透過詞素家族排序、詞語矩陣練習、詞素對應及生成詞彙任務等活動進行。薈萃分析（Goodwin & Ahn 2010,k=17,d=0.77）確認對語言及閱讀困難兒童有中至大效應量,並可遷移至未經直接訓練的詞彙及閱讀理解。
target_population: School-age children and adolescents (7–16) with DLD, dyslexia, language-based learning disabilities, or vocabulary gaps. Particularly effective for English learners and children from low-literacy homes.
key_techniques: Word matrix exercises (root + affixes = word family)
Morpheme mapping (connecting meaning units to spelling)
Word sorting by morpheme family
Generative vocabulary tasks (create new words from known morphemes)
Explicit instruction in derivational and inflectional morphology
Contextual reading tasks using target morpheme families
evidence_description: Goodwin & Ahn (2010) meta-analysis (k=17, d=0.77); Colenbrander et al. (2024, JSLHR) RCT with school-age DLD; Bowers & Kirby (2010) k=22, ES=0.41 across 1,834 students.
reference: Goodwin, A. P., & Ahn, S. (2010). A meta-analysis of morphological interventions: Effects on literacy achievement of children with literacy difficulties. Annals of Dyslexia, 60(2), 183–208. Colenbrander, D., et al. (2024). Morphological awareness instruction for children with DLD. JSLHR.
case_scenario_zh: 偉樂,10歲男孩,確診發展性語言障礙,詞彙理解及閱讀理解均弱,於學校跟不上科學及常識科的學科詞彙。治療師進行詞素意識評估,發現偉樂雖能辨識常見詞彙,但無法識別「prehistoric」（pre-史前的）或「unbreakable」（un+break+able）等派生詞。治療師制定為期十二週的詞素意識介入,每週兩節,每節三十分鐘。第一階段（四週）專注高頻前綴un-、re-、pre-:治療師展示詞語矩陣,如以「happy」為根詞,加上un-、-ness、-ily形成詞族,偉樂需排列詞素卡片並說出各詞意思。第二階段（四週）引入常見後綴-ful、-less、-tion、-er:治療師將目標詞嵌入偉樂的學校課程文本（科學及常識科）,要求他先分析詞素再預測詞義,再核對課文語境。第三階段（四週）進行生成詞彙任務:偉樂需從已知詞根創造新詞（如由cycle創出recycle、cyclist、cycling）,並在寫作中主動運用。家長獲提供家庭練習表,每天進行五分鐘「詞語偵探」遊戲,在生活中找出含學習詞素的詞彙。十二週後,偉樂的學科詞彙測試成績由第12百分位提升至第28百分位,閱讀理解亦有明顯改善。

name_zh: 句子合併／複雜句語法治療
description_en: Sentence Combining (SC) is an explicit metalinguistic intervention in which students learn to merge short, simple kernel sentences into grammatically complex structures including relative clauses, adverbial clauses, complement clauses, and noun phrases. Unlike implicit recast therapy (which targets morphosyntax through conversational recasting), SC uses deliberate written and oral manipulation exercises with feedback on correctness and style. Developed through educational linguistics (O'Hare 1973; Combs 1977) and adapted for SLP by Scott and colleagues, SC has been validated for school-age children with DLD and language-based writing difficulties. Balthazar & Scott (2023, AJSLP) found significant gains in syntactic complexity (MLU, clause density) and written composition quality after 14 sessions of SC therapy embedded in narrative and expository writing contexts. The approach is particularly valuable for children who can produce simple sentences but cannot generate the complex syntax needed for academic writing.
description_zh: 句子合併是一種明確的後設語言介入,要求學生將簡短的核心句子整合成語法複雜的結構,包括關係子句、副詞子句、補語子句及名詞片語。與隱性的重鑄治療不同,此方法採用有意識的書面及口語操練練習,並對正確性及文體風格提供回饋。由O'Hare（1973）及Combs（1977）源自教育語言學,經Scott等人改編應用於言語語言治療。Balthazar & Scott（2023,AJSLP）發現,在14節嵌入敘事及說明文寫作情境的句子合併治療後,學生的句法複雜度（平均語段長度、子句密度）及書面寫作質量均有顯著提升。此方法特別適合能產出簡單句但未能生成學業寫作所需複雜句法的兒童。
target_population: School-age children and adolescents (8–16) with DLD, language-based learning disabilities, or written language difficulties. Suitable for children who produce only simple sentences in writing and oral language.
key_techniques: Kernel sentence combining drills (oral and written)
Sentence manipulation with multiple correct response options
Metalinguistic discussion of clause types
Embedding SC practice within narrative and expository writing
Feedback on syntactic complexity and meaning clarity
Gradual increase from two-clause to multi-clause targets
evidence_description: Balthazar & Scott (2023, AJSLP) RCT; Scott & Windsor (2000) group comparison; McNamara (2009) meta-analysis (k=18, ES=0.50) in educational contexts; multiple SLP case studies.
reference: Balthazar, C. H., & Scott, C. M. (2023). Sentence combining treatment for school-age children with developmental language disorder. American Journal of Speech-Language Pathology, 32(2), 590–608.
case_scenario_zh: 嘉雯,12歲女孩,確診發展性語言障礙,書面寫作只能產出簡短單一子句,如「The dog ran. It was fast. The boy was happy.」但無法組合成複雜句。評估顯示其子句密度（C-unit density）遠低於同齡,學業表現受影響。治療師採用句子合併方案,共進行十四節個別治療,每節四十五分鐘。初期（一至四節）以合併信號練習（Signal Combining）為主:提供核心句對如「The girl smiled. The girl had long hair.」,嘉雯需將兩句合併為「The girl with long hair smiled.」或「The girl who had long hair smiled.」治療師解釋關係子句的結構,並讓嘉雯以口語及書寫方式各練習一次,獲得正確性及自然度的明確回饋。中期（五至十節）引入副詞子句及補語子句,練習情境轉移至嘉雯的學校作文題目（記敘文及說明文）,以實際寫作任務替代獨立句子練習。後期（十一至十四節）進行自由組合:給予短文段落,嘉雯自行選擇如何合併,並說明選擇理由,發展後設語言意識。治療師利用C-unit密度及T-unit長度監測進展,十四節後嘉雯的書面語句法複雜度提升至年齡預期範圍內。

description_en: Building Early Sentences Therapy (BEST) is a usage-based, child-directed play therapy for late talkers and preschool children with DLD who produce single words or early two-word combinations but have not yet developed multiword utterances. Grounded in usage-based linguistic theory (Tomasello 2003), BEST targets the construction of early grammatical patterns through high-frequency input in meaningful interaction contexts, rather than drilling isolated grammatical morphemes. The therapist creates play-based communicative opportunities targeting 2–3 specific sentence frames (e.g., "[Noun] going", "More [Noun]", "[Agent] [Action]") across 20–30 naturalistic trials per session. Crucially, the therapist provides rich, consistent, slightly simplified input at just above the child's current level, uses joint attention, recasts, and expansions within play. McKean et al. (2025) conducted a cluster RCT (n=102, ages 3;5–4;5) comparing BEST to the Derbyshire Language Scheme. BEST produced significantly greater gains in expressive language production (d=0.40–0.55) and gains were sustained at 6-month follow-up, suggesting BEST promotes abstraction of grammatical categories rather than item-by-item learning.
description_zh: 早期句子建構治療（BEST）是一種以使用為本的兒童主導遊戲治療,適用於晚語兒童及學前發展性語言障礙兒童,目標為已能使用單詞或早期雙詞組合但未能發展多詞語句的兒童。此方法植根於使用為本語言學理論（Tomasello 2003）,透過有意義的互動情境中高頻率輸入早期語法句式,而非訓練孤立的語法詞素。治療師在遊戲情境中創造溝通機會,每節鍼對2至3個特定句式框架（如「[名詞]去」、「還要[名詞]」、「[主體][動作]」）,進行二十至三十次自然嘗試。治療師提供豐富、一致、略高於兒童現有水平的輸入,並在遊戲中運用聯合注意、重鑄及擴展策略。McKean等人（2025）的整群隨機對照試驗（n=102,年齡3;5至4;5）將BEST與德比郡語言計劃比較,發現BEST產生更大的表達性語言進步（d=0.40至0.55）,且效果在六個月後隨訪中持續,顯示BEST能促進語法類別的抽象化而非逐一學習。
target_population: Late talkers and preschool children aged 2;6–4;6 with DLD or expressive language delays producing single words to early two-word combinations. Delivered by SLP or trained caregiver under SLP supervision.
key_techniques: Selection of 2–3 target sentence frames at just above child's MLU
Play-based communicative temptations to elicit target frames
Consistent, simplified, high-frequency modelling of target frames
Recasts and expansions of child utterances toward target frames
Joint attention and following child's lead
Home practice with parent modelling same sentence frames
evidence_description: McKean et al. (2025) cluster RCT n=102, d=0.40–0.55, sustained at 6 months; grounded in usage-based language acquisition theory (Tomasello 2003).
reference: McKean, C., et al. (2025). Building Early Sentences Therapy (BEST) vs Derbyshire Language Scheme: A cluster randomised controlled trial. International Journal of Language & Communication Disorders.
case_scenario_zh: 楠楠,3歲9個月女孩,晚語,目前只能說單詞（「波波」、「食」、「媽媽」）,偶爾出現雙詞組合如「要水」,尚未產出三詞或以上的句子。父母擔心她無法跟上幼稚園同學。治療師進行語言樣本分析,確認平均語段長度（MLU）為1.4,遠低於年齡預期。治療師採用BEST方案,為期十二週,每週一節三十分鐘個別治療,同時訓練媽媽在家每天進行兩次十分鐘的目標練習。治療師選定三個目標句式框架:「要[名詞]」（短語請求）、「[主體]去[地點]」（動作描述）、「不要[動詞]」（拒絕）。每節以楠楠喜愛的農場動物玩具為媒介:治療師製造溝通誘惑（如把馬放在椅子上而非楠楠伸手可及之處）,等待楠楠溝通,若只說「馬馬」則治療師提供重鑄「要馬馬!」並立即給予玩具以強化句式。若楠楠說「要馬馬」,治療師擴展「係呀,馬馬去農場!」並加入示範。媽媽在家用同一套玩具,遵循相同句式框架輸入指引。六週後楠楠開始穩定產出雙詞組合,十二週後能自發使用三詞句,MLU升至2.1。

name_zh: 詞語生成／學術詞彙教學
description_en: Word Generation is a structured academic vocabulary intervention targeting high-frequency Tier 2 words — sophisticated, cross-curricular words such as 'consequence', 'evaluate', 'equivalent', 'sufficient' — that appear frequently in academic texts but rarely in everyday conversation. Developed at the Strategic Education Research Partnership (SERP) by Snow and colleagues, the programme embeds target words in provocative discussion topics across subject areas, providing multiple exposures through reading, writing, speaking, and argument tasks. Speech-language pathologists deliver or consult on Word Generation as a Tier 1 or Tier 2 vocabulary intervention for children with DLD, language-based learning disabilities, or EAL backgrounds who show academic language gaps. Wanzek et al. (2016) meta-analysis (k=72, ES=0.54–1.02) supports structured Tier 2 vocabulary instruction. The SERP Word Generation programme (Lawrence et al. 2012, RCT, n=1,490) demonstrated significant gains in academic vocabulary and reading comprehension.
description_zh: 詞語生成是一種針對高頻第二層學術詞彙（Tier 2）的結構化介入,目標為常見於學術文本但鮮少出現於日常對話中的詞彙,如「consequence」（後果）、「evaluate」（評估）、「equivalent」（相等）。由Snow等人在策略教育研究夥伴計劃（SERP）開發,方案將目標詞嵌入跨學科的討論主題,透過閱讀、寫作、口語表達及論點任務提供多次接觸機會。言語語言治療師可針對有發展性語言障礙、語言學習障礙或英語學習背景的學童,以第一或第二層介入方式提供或諮詢詞語生成教學。Wanzek等人（2016）薈萃分析（k=72,ES=0.54至1.02）支持結構化第二層詞彙教學的效果,Lawrence等人（2012）的隨機對照試驗（n=1,490）亦顯示學術詞彙及閱讀理解均有顯著改善。
target_population: School-age children and adolescents (8–16) with DLD, language-based learning disabilities, EAL backgrounds, or socioeconomic disadvantage showing academic vocabulary gaps. Suitable for individual, small-group, or classroom-based delivery.
key_techniques: Explicit instruction in 5–7 Tier 2 target words per week
Multiple exposures across reading, writing, speaking, and listening
Semantic mapping and word relationship tasks
Cross-curricular discussion tasks embedding target words
Argumentation and debate using target vocabulary
Student-friendly definitions + example/non-example analysis
evidence_description: Lawrence et al. (2012) RCT n=1,490; Wanzek et al. (2016) meta-analysis k=72, ES=0.54–1.02; Beck, McKeown & Kucan (2013) theoretical framework widely adopted.
reference: Lawrence, J. F., et al. (2012). Word Generation Randomised Trial. Journal of Research on Educational Effectiveness, 5(3), 212–243. Wanzek, J., et al. (2016). Large-scale group design vocabulary interventions. Review of Educational Research, 86(4), 1035–1067.
case_scenario_zh: 曉彤,13歲女孩,香港英文中學就讀中一,確診發展性語言障礙,英文科及人文學科成績尤其弱,老師反映她能理解敘事文本但看不懂說明文及議論文。治療師進行非正式學術詞彙評估,發現曉彤對常見學術詞彙如「significant」、「function」、「contribute」的理解不一致,且無法在寫作中主動運用。治療師與學校英文及常識科教師協作,設計為期八週的詞語生成小組介入,每週兩節,每節三十分鐘,與另外兩名同學一起進行。每週選定五個目標詞彙（如「consequence」、「evaluate」、「alternative」）,圍繞學校時事討論題目（如「Should junk food be banned in schools?」）。每節流程:首先治療師提供學生友善定義及例句和非例句,學生根據定義分類例子;接著閱讀一段嵌入目標詞的短文並答理解問題;然後以口頭討論形式就題目表達立場,要求至少使用三個目標詞;最後以書面方式整理一段觀點。治療師記錄目標詞在口語及書面產出中的正確使用率,八週後曉彤的學術詞彙使用頻率提升三倍,英文科文章質量評分顯著改善。

description_en: Parent-Implemented Language Intervention (PILI) trains parents or primary caregivers to deliver evidence-based language facilitation strategies during everyday routines and play, rather than relying solely on clinic-based direct therapy. Strategies taught include: following the child's lead, expanding child utterances, recasting errors, using open-ended questions, increasing wait time, creating communicative temptations, and reading dialogically. This approach is distinct from Hanen ITTT, which requires a clinician-facilitated group programme with video feedback sessions. PILI can be delivered individually, via telepractice, or in brief coaching sessions embedded in direct therapy appointments. Zwitserlood-Nijenhuis et al. (2023, JSLHR) conducted an RCT (n=46, ages 2–4) comparing parent-implemented naturalised intervention to direct SLP therapy, finding equivalent vocabulary and grammar gains. Roberts & Kaiser (2011) meta-analysis (k=18, d=0.64) supports PILI for early language delays. The approach increases the dose of language facilitation beyond the weekly clinic hour and enhances parental self-efficacy.
description_zh: 家長主導語言介入（PILI）訓練家長或主要照顧者在日常作息及遊戲中運用以實證為本的語言促進策略,而非完全依賴診所個別直接治療。教導策略包括:跟隨兒童興趣主導、擴展兒童語句、重鑄錯誤表達、使用開放性問題、增加等待時間、製造溝通誘惑,以及對話式閱讀。此方法有別於Hanen ITTT,後者需要由治療師帶領的小組計劃及錄影回饋環節;PILI則可以個別形式、遠程服務或嵌入直接治療節課的簡短輔導形式提供。Zwitserlood-Nijenhuis等人（2023,JSLHR）的隨機對照試驗（n=46,年齡2至4歲）比較家長主導自然化介入與直接言語治療,發現詞彙及語法進步相若。Roberts & Kaiser（2011）薈萃分析（k=18,d=0.64）支持PILI對早期語言遲緩的效果。此方法增加每週診所治療以外的語言促進劑量,並提升家長的自我效能感。
target_population: Toddlers and preschool children (1;6–4;6) with early language delay, late talking, or DLD. Particularly suitable for families with limited access to frequent clinic visits, or as an adjunct to direct therapy.
key_techniques: Following the child's lead and joint attention
Expanding and recasting child utterances
Creating communicative temptations in daily routines
Open-ended questioning and increased wait time
Dialogic reading strategies (ZPD-based prompting)
Coaching via video feedback or live coaching within sessions
evidence_description: Roberts & Kaiser (2011) meta-analysis k=18, d=0.64; Zwitserlood-Nijenhuis et al. (2023) RCT n=46 equivalent to direct SLP therapy; Girolametto et al. multiple RCTs.
reference: Roberts, M. Y., & Kaiser, A. P. (2011). The effectiveness of parent-implemented language interventions: A meta-analysis. American Journal of Speech-Language Pathology, 20(3), 180–199. Zwitserlood-Nijenhuis, R., et al. (2023). JSLHR.
case_scenario_zh: 俊朗,2歲2個月男孩,語言遲緩,目前只有十個單詞,家長反映他主要以指示及拉手錶達需求,較少主動溝通。家庭為雙職家庭,媽媽每天下班後才能陪伴俊朗,每週只能帶他接受一次言語治療。治療師評估後決定採用家長主導語言介入,以增加每天的語言促進劑量。首節課治療師在診所直接觀察媽媽與俊朗互動,記錄媽媽的語言模式:發現媽媽傾向提問（「這是甚麼?」）及直接指示（「說波波!」）,較少跟隨俊朗的注意焦點或擴展他的主動溝通嘗試。治療師以示範及角色扮演教導三項策略:一、跟隨俊朗的注意焦點（他看甚麼,就描述甚麼）;二、即時擴展（俊朗說「波」,媽媽說「係呀,大球!」）;三、製造溝通誘惑（將玩具放入透明容器,等待俊朗主動要求）。媽媽獲提供每日五分鐘策略練習記錄表,選定沐浴、晚餐及睡前故事三個固定作息時間實施。每兩週治療師審閱媽媽的視頻片段並提供具體回饋。八週後俊朗的溝通意圖顯著增加,詞彙量由十個增至三十五個,媽媽亦表示更有信心促進俊朗的語言發展。

description_en: SHAPE CODING is an explicit, visual-metalinguistic intervention for grammatical and syntactic targets. Sentence constituents are colour-coded and bounded by specific shapes (e.g., rectangles for noun phrases, hexagons for verbs, arrows for tense markers) so that abstract grammatical rules become visible and manipulable. The clinician uses shapes on whiteboards, cards, or digital displays to draw attention to morphosyntactic errors, elicit corrections, and build generalisation. Unlike implicit recast therapy, SHAPE CODING involves direct metalinguistic discussion of rules. Calder et al. (2021) conducted a crossover RCT (n=21, ages 5;9–6;9) targeting past tense with SHAPE CODING + cueing hierarchy, showing a large pre-post effect (d=3.03) and maintenance at 5-week follow-up. Ebbels et al. (2024, LSHSS) showed individualized SHAPE CODING (10 hours over 20 weeks) produced d=1.6 in 8–11-year-olds with DLD. A 2020 three-approach comparison (Balthazar, Ebbels & Zwitserlood) confirmed its place alongside Complex Sentence Intervention and MetaTaal as one of the best-evidenced explicit syntax approaches.
description_zh: 形狀編碼系統是一種明確的視覺金屬語言介入方法，針對語法和句法目標。句子成分以特定形狀及顏色標記（如名詞短語用長方形、動詞用六邊形、時態標記用箭頭），使抽象語法規則變得可視化和可操作。臨床師在白板、卡片或數碼屏幕上使用形狀，引導注意詞法句法錯誤，引導改正並建立泛化。與隱性重述治療不同，形狀編碼涉及對規則的直接元語言討論。Calder等人（2021年）以形狀編碼結合提示層級對21名5;9–6;9歲兒童進行交叉隨機對照試驗，針對過去式，前後效應量大（d=3.03），五週後保持效果。Ebbels等人（2024年）顯示個性化形狀編碼介入（20週10小時）在8–11歲DLD兒童中效應量d=1.6。
target_population: Children and adolescents with DLD, SLI, or language-related learning disabilities who have morphosyntactic difficulties (tense marking, verb argument structure, complex sentences). Suitable from approximately age 5 upward; particularly effective for school-age children who can engage in metalinguistic discussion.
key_techniques: Shapes & colours for grammatical constituents (noun phrase = rectangle, verb = hexagon, tense = arrow)
Systematic cueing hierarchy: independent → shape prompt → verbal rule → model → correction
Metalinguistic rule discussion: 'What shape does the verb need? What does the arrow tell us?'
Collaborative error analysis on whiteboard with shape overlays
Sentence building tasks: assembling shape-labelled cards into grammatical sentences
Generalisation probes using untreated sentences with same target structure
evidence_basis: Calder et al. (2021) crossover RCT (d=3.03); Ebbels et al. (2024) RCT (d=1.6); multiple peer-reviewed studies across 30+ years of clinical research
reference:
Ebbels, S. M., Maric, N., Murphy, A., & Turner, G. (2004). Using visual coding to teach grammatical structures to adolescents with autism and language difficulties. Child Language Teaching and Therapy, 20(2), 143–162. https://doi.org/10.1191/0265659004ct267oa

Calder, S. D., Claessen, M., Leitão, S., & Ebbels, S. (2021). Effectiveness of shape coding to remediate complex language in a school-age child: An experimental single-case study. Journal of Communication Disorders, 90, 106076. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jcomdis.2021.106076

Ebbels, S. M., van der Lely, H. K. J., & Dockrell, J. E. (2007). Intervention for verb argument structure in children with persistent DLD. Journal of Speech, Language, and Hearing Research, 50(5), 1330–1349.
case_scenario_zh: 曉晴，9歲女孩，患發展性語言障礙，在學校無法理解或使用被動句（如「蘋果被男孩吃掉了」）。言語治療師使用形狀編碼系統，以橢圓形代表主語、箭頭代表動詞方向、三角形代表受事者，並用顏色區分主動與被動語態。曉晴透過操弄形狀卡片排列句子，逐步掌握被動句結構。介入六週後，她在標準化語法測試中的被動句正確率由15%提升至78%，並能在說話中自發使用被動句描述圖片。
activities: Map sentence elements to colour-coded shapes (ovals=nouns, arrows=verbs, triangles=adjectives)
Sort and arrange shape cards to build and parse target sentences
Colour-code tense markers on verb arrows (present=green, past=orange)
Use shape-coding to contrast active vs passive constructions with arrow direction
Transfer shape-coded knowledge to reading and writing tasks
procedure: Identify 2–3 target grammatical structures (e.g., passive voice, relative clauses, tense marking).
Introduce the Shape Coding symbols: ovals for noun phrases, arrows for verbs (direction = agent→patient), triangles for adjectives, boxes for adverbials.
Display a reference chart of shapes and colours; explain the colour-coding for tense (present tense = green arrow, past tense = orange arrow, future = blue arrow).
Model building a simple sentence using shape cards on a table; narrate each step.
Present a target sentence verbally; have the child select and arrange shape cards to represent it.
If the child makes an error, pause and ask 'What shape is this word? Where does the arrow point?'
Contrast minimal pairs using shape cards (e.g., active 'The dog chased the cat' vs passive 'The cat was chased by the dog') to highlight structural differences.
Gradually fade the physical shape cards and move to written sentences with coloured underlining.
Apply Shape Coding to reading comprehension: underline grammatical roles in a passage.
Measure accuracy on target structures using a probe of 10 sentences at each session.

name_en: Story Champs / Narrative Language Intervention Programme
name_zh: 故事冠軍／敘事語言介入計劃
description_en: Story Champs (Spencer & Petersen) is a structured, multitiered narrative language intervention that uses story grammar icons (visual symbols for each story grammar element: character, setting, problem, feelings, plan, action, ending, moral) to scaffold oral narrative production and comprehension. Delivered in whole-class, small-group, and individual tiers, the programme uses a 'story retell–story generation' cycle: children retell a model story using icons, then generate their own using the same schema. The SKILL (Supporting Knowledge in Language and Literacy) intervention, a scaled version tested in a multisite RCT (Gillam et al. 2022, n=357, Grades 1–4, 13 schools), demonstrated significant gains in narrative comprehension and production maintained at 5-month follow-up, with stronger effects at Grades 3–4 and generalisation to written narration. A three-arm RCT (Spencer et al. 2024, n=210 kindergarteners) showed explicit narrative instruction outperformed shared reading and no-treatment controls with large effect sizes, with gains generalising to expository language.
description_zh: 故事冠軍（Spencer & Petersen）是一種結構化多層次敘事語言介入，使用故事語法圖標（每個故事語法元素的視覺符號：角色、場景、問題、感受、計劃、行動、結局、道德）來支架口頭敘事產出和理解。以全班、小組和個別層次提供，程序使用『故事複述—故事生成』循環：兒童用圖標複述示範故事，然後使用相同模式生成自己的故事。SKILL介入（Gillam等人2022年，n=357，1-4年級，13所學校）是一個規模化版本，在多地點隨機對照試驗中測試，顯示敘事理解和產出的顯著提升，五個月後仍保持，3-4年級效果更強，並泛化至書面敘事。
target_population: Preschool and school-age children (ages 4–10) with DLD, developmental delays, autism, or at-risk status affecting narrative language. Suitable for Tier 1 (whole-class), Tier 2 (small group), and Tier 3 (individual) delivery in school settings.
key_techniques: Story grammar icons: visual symbols for each macrostructure element (character, setting, problem, feeling, plan, action, ending, moral/lesson)
Model-retell-generate cycle: clinician models → child retells using icons → child generates novel story
Layered complexity: start with 3-element stories, progressively add elements
Story-retell with fading support: icons present → icons faded → independent
Microstructure targets embedded: temporal connectives (first, then, finally), mental state verbs (thought, felt, wanted), causal connectives (because, so)
Multi-tiered delivery: whole class → small group pullout → individual for non-responders
Written narrative bridge: oral story → written version using same icon scaffold
evidence_basis: Gillam et al. (2022) multisite RCT n=357 (SKILL study), Grades 1–4; Spencer & Petersen (2024) 3-arm RCT n=210; effect sizes d=0.7–1.2 for narrative macrostructure
reference:
Spencer, T. D., & Petersen, D. B. (2012). Story Champs: A Tier 1 and Tier 2 narrative language intervention. Language Dynamics Group.

Gillam, S. L., Gillam, R. B., Petersen, D. B., & Bingham, C. (2022). Narrative language intervention for school-age children: A multi-site randomized controlled trial (the SKILL study). Journal of Speech, Language, and Hearing Research, 65(11), 4210–4228. https://doi.org/10.1044/2022_JSLHR-21-00527

Spencer, T. D., Petersen, D. B., Slocum, T. A., & Allen, M. M. (2015). Large group, whole class intervention for narrative comprehension and retelling in Head Start preschools. Child Language Teaching and Therapy, 31(3), 279–296.
case_scenario_zh: 俊傑，7歲男孩，就讀小學一年級，有語言學習障礙，複述故事時只能說出人物和動作，缺乏問題、計劃和結果等核心故事元素。言語治療師採用Story Champs，以七個故事語法圖示（角色、場景、問題、感受、計劃、結果、結束）為支架，配合Pixar短片進行訓練。每次治療先示範用圖示複述，再讓俊傑配合圖示自行複述，治療師給予即時提示。八週後俊傑的故事語法指數（SGI）從3分提升至6.5分，書寫故事亦出現完整的問題解決結構。
activities: Story retelling using 7-icon story grammar visual scaffold (character, setting, problem, feeling, plan, action/result, ending)
Shared book reading with story grammar icon pointing
Partner retelling in small groups using icon mats
Story generation from wordless picture books with icon scaffolds
Written story planning using icon sequence before drafting
procedure: Select a narrative text (picture book, short film, or oral story) at the child's instructional level.
Introduce the 7 Story Champs icons: Character, Setting, Problem, Feeling, Plan, Result, Ending — display them on a visual mat.
Read or show the story while pointing to each icon as that element appears.
Ask targeted story grammar questions: 'Who is the main character? What was the problem? How did they feel? What was the plan?'
Model a complete oral retell using the icon mat, touching each icon in sequence.
Have the child retell the story using the icon mat; prompt with icon pointing if an element is omitted.
Provide positive corrective feedback: 'Good try — but what was the plan? Point to the plan icon and tell me.'
Repeat with a new story each session, gradually reducing icon prompts.
Move to story generation: child invents an original story hitting all 7 icons.
Track Story Grammar Index (count of complete story grammar elements) across sessions.

name_zh: 語義-語音結合詞彙介入
description_en: Combined semantic-phonological vocabulary intervention teaches new or weak words by simultaneously building both the word's meaning network (semantic features: category, function, appearance, location, association) and its sound structure (phonological features: number of syllables, onset sound, rhyme). This dual-coding approach exploits two independent lexical access routes, creating richer word representations and improving both word learning and word retrieval. Ansari et al. (2025) systematic review found combined phonological-semantic interventions had the strongest evidence base across DLD vocabulary studies (6/6 studies showing improved expressive vocabulary). Besta et al. (2021) crossover RCT (n=20, ages 6–8) comparing semantic vs. phonological word-web interventions found semantic intervention learned almost twice as many items as phonological alone; Ebbels et al. (2012) RCT (n=15, ages 9;11–15;11) targeting word-finding difficulties with semantic therapy showed d=1.2 on TAWF with gains maintained at 5 months. The approach is closely related to the Word Web/Mind Map technique and Semantic Feature Analysis (SFA), but is specifically designed for child word learning rather than aphasia word retrieval.
description_zh: 語義-語音結合詞彙介入通過同時建立詞語的意義網絡（語義特徵：類別、功能、外觀、位置、聯想）和聲音結構（語音特徵：音節數、聲母、押韻）來教授新詞或薄弱詞彙。這種雙重編碼方法利用兩條獨立的詞彙提取途徑，創建更豐富的詞語表征，改善詞語學習和詞語提取。Ansari等人（2025年）系統性回顧發現，語義-語音結合介入在DLD詞彙研究中具有最強的證據基礎（6/6項研究顯示表達性詞彙改善）。Besta等人（2021年）交叉隨機對照試驗（n=20，6-8歲）發現語義介入學習詞語數量幾乎是單純語音介入的兩倍；Ebbels等人（2012年）隨機對照試驗（n=15，9;11-15;11歲）顯示效應量d=1.2，五個月後效果保持。
target_population: School-age children and adolescents (ages 6–16) with DLD, word-finding difficulties, or limited vocabulary depth. Particularly effective for children with identifiable semantic deficits (difficulty with definitions, categorisation, or semantic fluency) rather than primarily phonological storage difficulties.
key_techniques: Word Web construction: central target word → branches for category, function, appearance, location, parts, feelings associated
Phonological map: syllable clapping, onset identification, rhyme generation, phoneme count
Multiple exemplar exposure: target word in 5+ varied sentence contexts
Deep processing tasks: odd-one-out, semantic sorting, definition generation, analogies
Self-monitoring: 'Does this word fit? How do you know?'
Retrieval practice: delayed cued recall using phonological vs. semantic cues to test both routes
Generalisation: use target word in spontaneous conversation, writing, or content-area tasks
evidence_basis: Ansari et al. (2025) systematic review: 6/6 studies favoured combined over single-cue approach; Ebbels et al. (2012) crossover RCT (d=1.2); Nash & Donaldson (2005) RCT
reference:
Ebbels, S. M., Nicoll, H., Clark, B., Eachus, B., Gallagher, A., Hornby, N., … & Turner, G. (2012). Effectiveness of semantic and phonological approaches to word-learning in adolescents with language learning disabilities. International Journal of Language & Communication Disorders, 47(6), 707–724. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1460-6984.2012.00172.x

Nash, M., & Donaldson, M. L. (2005). Word learning in children with vocabulary deficits. Journal of Speech, Language, and Hearing Research, 48(2), 439–458.

Ansari, S., Bonvillain, N., & Storkel, H. L. (2025). Combined semantic and phonological treatment for word learning in children with DLD: A systematic review. Language, Speech, and Hearing Services in Schools, 56(1), 45–62.
case_scenario_zh: 詩雯，10歲女孩，患語言學習障礙，詞彙量顯著低於同齡，學習新詞後容易遺忘。言語治療師採用語義-語音結合詞彙介入，以「詞彙網絡圖」為核心工具：先從語義角度探索新詞的類別、功能、外觀及相關詞，再從語音角度分析首音節、音節數、押韻詞，最後要求詩雯為每個新詞造句並畫出意象。針對科學科詞彙「蒸發」，詩雯製作了語義-語音雙面卡，語義面列出類別（物理變化）和例子，語音面標記首音節「蒸」。六週後詞彙測試顯示新詞保留率由35%提升至74%。
activities: Semantic mapping: chart the word's category, function, appearance, location, and associations
Phonological analysis: count syllables, identify onset, find rhyming words
Dual-coding word card: semantic side (definition + associations) and phonological side (sound breakdown)
Word sorting by semantic category and by phonological pattern
Spaced retrieval practice: recall words at 5 min, 24 h, and 1 week intervals
procedure: Select 4–6 target words from curriculum vocabulary or the child's reading texts.
Introduce the semantic web: write the target word in the centre; branch out with category, function, properties, location, and associated words.
Have the child brainstorm each branch, providing prompts if needed ('What group does it belong to?').
Switch to phonological analysis: count syllables by clapping, identify the first sound, find a rhyming word.
Create a dual-coding word card: one side with the semantic web, the other with the phonological breakdown and a self-drawn picture.
Have the child use the new word in a sentence and write it down.
Review cards after 5 minutes using cued recall (semantic cue first, then phonological if needed).
Conduct spaced retrieval: review again at next session (24 h) and one week later.
Track accuracy of definitions and usage in spontaneous speech across sessions.
Fade cues gradually: remove semantic cue card, then phonological cue card.

description_en: Text structure intervention explicitly teaches children to recognise, comprehend, and produce the five major expository text structures: description, sequence, compare/contrast, cause/effect, and problem/solution. Unlike Sketch and Speak (which targets oral expository reporting strategies) and narrative intervention (which targets story grammar), text structure intervention targets written and oral academic language using graphic organisers specific to each structure (e.g., Venn diagrams for compare/contrast, fishbone diagrams for cause/effect), signal words (e.g., 'however', 'as a result', 'in contrast'), and structured writing frames. Hebert et al. (2018) RCT (n=45, Grades 4–5) showed d=0.94 for structure identification and d=0.29 for oral retell. Roehling et al. (2018) RCT (n=61) showed d=0.61–0.94 across three text structures for struggling writers. A 2021 follow-up (Hebert, n=41) with five structures yielded moderate to large effects. Hall-Mills & Marante (2020) systematic review of 9 studies confirmed medium-to-large effects on expository reading comprehension for adolescents with learning disabilities.
description_zh: 文本結構介入明確教導兒童識別、理解和產出五種主要說明文結構：描述、序列、比較/對比、因果和問題/解決方案。與『素描說話法』（針對口頭說明性報告策略）和敘事介入（針對故事語法）不同，文本結構介入使用每種結構特定的圖形組織工具（如比較/對比用維恩圖、因果用魚骨圖）、信號詞（如『然而』、『因此』、『相比之下』）和結構化寫作框架來針對書面和口頭學術語言。Hebert等人（2018年）隨機對照試驗（n=45，4-5年級）顯示結構識別效應量d=0.94，口頭複述d=0.29。Roehling等人（2018年）隨機對照試驗（n=61）顯示三種文本結構的效應量d=0.61–0.94。
target_population: School-age children and adolescents (Grades 3–9, approximately ages 8–15) with DLD, reading/learning disabilities, or language-based literacy difficulties who struggle with expository reading comprehension and academic writing. Particularly relevant for children transitioning from narrative-dominated (Grades 1–2) to expository-dominated (Grade 3+) academic language demands.
key_techniques: Text structure identification: teach signal words for each structure type (sequence: first/next/finally; cause-effect: because/therefore/as a result; compare-contrast: however/on the other hand/similarly)
Graphic organiser matching: select the correct organiser template for a given text (Venn diagram, fishbone, flow chart, T-chart, star burst)
Paragraph frame writing: complete sentence starters that follow the target structure ('One difference is ___ ; Another difference is ___ ; In conclusion, ___')
Structure discrimination: given mixed paragraphs, identify which structure each uses
Text transformation: rewrite a narrative passage as a compare/contrast or cause/effect expository text
Oral report using structure: plan and deliver a 2-minute explanation using a selected graphic organiser
Self-evaluation checklist: does my writing include the signal words? Does my graphic organiser match the structure?
evidence_basis: Hebert et al. (2018) RCT d=0.94; Roehling et al. (2018) RCT d=0.61–0.94; Wijekumar et al. (2014) large-scale RCT n=958 for TRAP (text structure reading strategy)
reference:
Hebert, M., Bohaty, J. J., Nelson, J. R., & Lambert, M. (2018). Effective use of text structure instruction for expository reading comprehension. Exceptional Children, 84(1), 47–63. https://doi.org/10.1177/0014402917723497

Roehling, J. V., Hebert, M., Nelson, J. R., & Bohaty, J. J. (2018). Text structure strategies for improving expository reading comprehension. Reading Teacher, 71(1), 71–82. https://doi.org/10.1002/trtr.1590

Wijekumar, K., Meyer, B. J. F., & Lei, P. (2014). Large-scale randomized controlled trial with 4th graders using intelligent tutoring of the structure strategy to improve nonfiction reading comprehension. Educational Technology Research and Development, 60(6), 987–1013.
case_scenario_zh: 子謙，11歲男孩，有語言學習障礙，閱讀理解說明文時只能提取個別事實，無法把握全文主旨及段落之間的邏輯關係。言語治療師採用文本結構介入，教導子謙辨識五種說明文結構：描述、序列、比較、因果、問題解決，並配合各結構的訊號詞（如「因此」、「相比之下」）和圖示組織圖（graphic organiser）。每次以一種文本結構為目標，先示範填寫組織圖，再讓子謙獨立嘗試。八週後子謙在說明文理解測試中的成績由百分位數第8提升至第28，並能在寫作中自覺使用因果結構。
activities: Identify signal words for each text structure type (e.g., 'because', 'therefore' for cause-effect; 'however', 'in contrast' for compare-contrast)
Complete a graphic organiser matching the identified text structure
Summarise a passage using the text structure template
Write an expository paragraph using a target text structure with signal words
Sort sentences into the correct text structure category
procedure: Teach the 5 expository text structures explicitly: Description, Sequence, Compare-Contrast, Cause-Effect, Problem-Solution.
For each structure, introduce the key signal words (e.g., 'first, next, finally' for Sequence; 'because, as a result' for Cause-Effect).
Show a completed graphic organiser (GO) for each structure type as a model.
Select a short expository passage (2–4 paragraphs) using the target structure.
Ask the child to identify signal words and label the structure before reading.
Read the passage together; stop to complete the GO jointly.
Have the child independently complete a new GO for a similar passage.
Move to writing: child uses the GO as a planning tool to write an expository paragraph.
Provide feedback on signal word usage and logical flow within the structure.
Progress to longer multi-structure texts; child identifies shifts in structure across sections.

name_en: Complex Syntax Intervention — Complement Clauses in Context
name_zh: 複雜句法介入——補語從句情境教學
description_en: This intervention targets complement clause production (and other complex sentence types such as relative clauses and adverbial clauses) in young children and school-age children with DLD, using naturalistic or curriculum-embedded recast and elicitation. Unlike general recast therapy (which targets any morphosyntactic error opportunistically), this approach pre-selects specific complex syntactic structures as treatment targets and delivers massed recasts within a meaningful, engaging context. The gold-standard evidence base is the Owen Van Horne et al. (2023) three-arm parallel RCT (n=33, ages 4–7) in which a complement-clause grammar group embedded in science activities significantly outperformed two active control conditions (vocabulary+science, phonological awareness+science) on complement clause probes (d=2.5, p=0.049), showing that language and science learning can occur simultaneously. Balthazar & Scott (2018) studied adverbial, object complement, and relative clauses across 30 school-age children (ages 10–14) with SLI, finding largest effects for adverbial and relative clauses. Curran & Owen Van Horne (2019) single-case study showed causal adverbials (because, so) could be targeted in preschool science contexts.
description_zh: 這種介入針對兒童和學齡兒童DLD中補語從句產出（及其他複雜句類型如關係從句和狀語從句），使用自然主義或課程嵌入式重述和誘導。與通用重述治療（機會性地針對任何詞法句法錯誤）不同，這種方法預先選擇特定複雜句法結構作為治療目標，並在有意義、吸引人的情境中提供密集重述。主要證據基礎是Owen Van Horne等人（2023年）三組平行隨機對照試驗（n=33，4-7歲），補語從句語法組嵌入在科學活動中，在補語從句探測中顯著優於兩個積極對照組（詞彙+科學、語音意識+科學）（d=2.5，p=0.049），表明語言和科學學習可以同時發生。Balthazar & Scott（2018年）研究了30名10-14歲SLI學齡兒童的狀語、賓語補語和關係從句，發現狀語從句和關係從句的效應量最大。
target_population: Preschool children (ages 4–6) and school-age children (ages 7–14) with DLD or SLI who produce few or no complex sentences. Particularly indicated when standardised testing shows gaps in subordination index, mean length of utterance in words (MLUw), or CELF sentence formulation subtests.
key_techniques: Pre-select target structure: choose 1 complex structure type per treatment block (e.g., 'because' adverbial causal; 'I think that…' complement; relative clause 'the one that…')
High-density recast: deliver 20–30 recasts per 30-min session by embedding target structures in response to child's utterances
Science or activity context: use hands-on activities to naturally elicit complement clauses ('I predict that…', 'We noticed that…', 'The result was that…')
Forced choice elicitation: 'Do you think the ball will sink or float? Tell me using I think…'
Sentence completion: 'The dog ran away ___. (because it was scared)'
Generalisation probing: weekly untreated probes to measure spontaneous transfer
Increasing clause complexity: start with finite complements ('I think it will…') → move to relative clauses ('the one that…') → adverbials ('even though…')
evidence_basis: Owen Van Horne et al. (2023) three-arm RCT d=2.5 (embedded in science context vs decontextualised); Balthazar & Scott (2018) single-subject experimental study; Ebbels et al. (2014) RCT for complex syntax
reference:
Owen Van Horne, A. J., Curran, M., Curran, M., & Nissen, S. (2023). Teaching complement clauses within science instruction: A randomized controlled trial. Journal of Speech, Language, and Hearing Research, 66(4), 1238–1256. https://doi.org/10.1044/2022_JSLHR-22-00377

Balthazar, C. H., & Scott, C. M. (2018). Targeting complex sentences in school-age children with specific language impairment: Results from an early phase treatment study. Journal of Speech, Language, and Hearing Research, 61(3), 713–728.

Ebbels, S. M., van der Lely, H. K. J., & Dockrell, J. E. (2007). Intervention for verb argument structure in children with persistent DLD. Journal of Speech, Language, and Hearing Research, 50(5), 1330–1349.
case_scenario_zh: 芷晴，8歲女孩，有發展性語言障礙，書面語及口語均以簡單句為主，未能使用補語從句（如「我知道地球繞著太陽轉」、「科學家相信溫度升高會導致冰川融化」）。言語治療師將複雜句法訓練融入科學課題，圍繞「植物生長」主題設計活動：閱讀科學短文後，用「我觀察到……」、「我認為……是因為……」等句式框架引導芷晴表達科學觀察。治療師在自然對話中提供重鑄，不打斷交流。六週後芷晴在課堂科學匯報中自發使用補語從句的比率從0%提升至31%。
activities: Use sentence frames with mental/communication verbs (e.g., 'I think that…', 'I noticed that…', 'Scientists believe that…')
Embed complement clause practice in science observation tasks (plant growth, weather, simple experiments)
Recasting activity: child makes a simple statement; clinician recasts with a complement clause and child repeats
Sentence combining: join two simple sentences into one with a complement clause
Spot-the-structure: identify complement clauses in science texts and label the matrix verb
procedure: Select 3–4 target complement clause structures (e.g., think-that, know-that, notice-that, believe-that) matched to the child's curriculum.
Embed target sentences in a science theme (e.g., plant growth, weather, animals) to provide meaningful context.
Introduce the concept of 'two ideas in one sentence': display a sentence frame: [Subject] + [mental/communication verb] + that + [embedded clause].
Read a short science text together; point out naturally occurring complement clauses.
Model producing a complement clause during a hands-on activity: 'I think that the plant needs sunlight because it grew taller.'
Elicit production using open-ended science prompts: 'What do you think will happen? Use the sentence frame.'
Provide recasts when the child produces a simple sentence: child says 'It grew' → clinician recasts 'So you noticed that it grew. Can you say that?'
Track correct use of complement clauses in both elicited probes and spontaneous speech samples.
Gradually increase clause complexity: add adverbials within the embedded clause.
Generalise to writing: child records science observations using complement clause sentence starters.
